message:
fix(malloc): remove bump-pointer entries from fallback tracker on free/realloc

After the prior change taught fallback_insert_sized to track
bump-arena allocations (so the unistd EFAULT guard could see their
remaining capacity), the matching teardown paths kept ignoring bump
pointers entirely. Two leak/aliasing windows resulted:

* free(bump_ptr) returned without consulting the fallback tracker,
  so the recorded length entry survived the free. A subsequent
  malloc that reused the same bump cell would see the stale entry
  on the first known_remaining lookup, returning the OLD remaining
  count for the new allocation.

* realloc(bump_ptr, _) similarly reinserted the new pointer's
  length without removing the old one. Two simultaneous entries
  for overlapping addresses left fallback_contains positive even
  for the freed range.

Both call sites now invoke fallback_remove(ptr) before the
existing branch ends — the bump-arena free path adds the discard,
and the realloc bump path adds it just before fallback_insert_sized
so the new pointer's record is the only live entry.

The discard return values are explicitly ignored (`let _ = ...`)
because removing a never-tracked pointer is a no-op in this
tracker — preserving idempotence, matching the existing pattern
on the heap free path.
